title: Infer hidden Safety Car/VSC windows from fieldwide lap shocks
summary: Official F1 strategy material emphasizes that Safety Car/VSC periods sharply reduce pit-loss and change stop timing, but the dataset appears to lack an explicit flag.
rationale: Official F1 strategy material emphasizes that Safety Car/VSC periods sharply reduce pit-loss and change stop timing, but the dataset appears to lack an explicit flag. A race-lap latent 'neutralization' proxy from simultaneous fieldwide lap-time inflation could recover that missing state.
implementation_hint: Within each Year-Race-LapNumber, compute robust field statistics such as median LapTime_Delta, IQR, fraction of drivers with extreme positive delta, and sudden compression in position changes. Feed these race-lap features to the main model and interact them with PitStop, TyreLife, Laps_Remaining_Est, and wet/dry compound flags.
expected_effect: Should improve recall on opportunistic stops and reduce false negatives around abnormal race-control windows where current pointwise tyre heuristics are blind.
risk: Can overfit to race-specific artifacts if validation leaks race-lap context across folds; use whole-race holdouts when testing.
